key_insight: For shallow ReLU^k networks, the paper identifies the exact function space behind ridge-integral representations: in L2 it matches a critical Sobolev space, and in Lp it sits in a sharp Sobolev sandwich whose gap is governed by Radon-transform smoothing/loss.
engineering_relevance: This gives a principled way to predict approximation quality and sample complexity for linearized neural networks, especially when choosing activation smoothness and target regularity in high-dimensional settings.
method: Define a Radon-domain L^p space for coefficient densities in ridge-integral representations of ReLU^k networks.
Use elementary Fourier analysis to prove the L2 identity with H^{k+(d+1)/2}(Ω) and establish the sharp Sobolev sandwich for 1<p<∞.
Discretize the integral model with a deterministic interpolation skeleton plus uniform sampling to obtain high-probability approximation bounds.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports theoretical proofs and a discretization analysis that yields high-probability L^p rates, including the optimal Hilbert-space rate O(n^{-1/2-(2k+1)/(2d)}) at p=2.
watch_item: The sharp results rely on bounded-domain assumptions and Radon-transform operator theory, so practical performance may depend on how well a finite-width implementation approximates the continuous ridge-integral model.
paper_title: Sharp Sobolev Sandwich and Approximation Rates of Radon-Domain $L^p$ Ridge Integral Spaces for ReLU$^k$ Networks
